title: 10 best herbal capsules for hypertension
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for hypertension are dietary supplements that contain a combination of herbs and plant extracts, which are believed to help manage high blood pressure. These natural alternatives can be a viable option for individuals seeking to control their hypertension without relying solely on prescription medications. Some examples of herbal ingredients used in these capsules include hawthorn, garlic, fish oil, and coenzyme Q10. These herbs work by promoting vasodilation, reducing inflammation, and improving endothelial function, all of which contribute to lowering blood pressure. By incorporating herbal capsules into their treatment plan, many people have experienced an improvement in their overall health and well-being, as well as a reduced risk of hypertension-related complications such as stroke and heart disease.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic capsule helps with hypertension because it contains allicin, a compound that has been shown to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, which can help reduce high blood pressure. Additionally, garlic has been found to have a mild anticoagulant effect, which can help prevent the formation of blood clots and may contribute to its ability to lower blood pressure. Furthermore, garlic capsules can provide a consistent and convenient dosage of garlic, making it easier for individuals to incorporate this beneficial herb into their daily routine for managing hypertension.
remedy_properties: Vasodilation: Garlic capsules contain allicin, which is converted to nitric oxide in the body, causing blood vessels to widen and reducing blood pressure.
Antiplatelet activity: Garlic capsules contain ajoene, which inhibits platelet aggregation, reducing the risk of blood clots and improving blood flow in hypertensive individuals.

Antioxidant properties: Garlic capsules contain polyphenols and flavonoids, which scavenge free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, contributing to better endothelial function and blood pressure control.
Diuretic effect: Garlic capsules promote diuresis by increasing renal blood flow and sodium excretion, helping to lower blood pressure in hypertensive patients.

Inhibition of angiotensin-converting enzyme: Garlic capsules contain S-allylcysteine, which inhibits ACE, reducing the formation of angiotensin II, a potent vasoconstrictor, and thereby lowering blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Bulbs: Garlic bulbs are commonly used to make medicinal capsules for hypertension due to their high concentration of allicin, a compound known for its blood pressure-lowering properties.

Leaves: While not as potent as the bulbs, garlic leaves can still be used in medicinal capsules for hypertension, as they contain beneficial compounds that contribute to the plant's overall cardiovascular benefits.
Flowers: Garlic flowers contain beneficial compounds, including allicin, and can be used in medicinal capsules for hypertension, although they are less potent than the bulbs.
Seeds: Garlic seeds, also known as cloves, are the most commonly used part of the garlic plant for medicinal purposes, as they are rich in allicin and other compounds that help lower blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Crush 4 cloves of garlic and 2 tablespoons of dried herbs (such as hawthorn, motherwort, and mistletoe) into a fine paste.
Mix the paste with 1 cup of warm water and let it sit for 30 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth, pressing on the solids to extract all the liquid.
Combine the liquid with 1/2 cup of tapioca starch and heat over low heat for 15 minutes, stirring constantly, until it forms a thick paste.
Roll the paste into small capsules and let them dry for 24 hours before storing in a cool, dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: European mistletoe capsule helps with hypertension because it contains natural compounds that have been shown to dilate blood vessels and regulate blood pressure. The herb contains various pharmacologically active constituents, such as viscotoxins, lectins, and flavonoids, which can help improve circulation, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation of the cardiovascular system. Regular intake of European mistletoe capsules may help lower high blood pressure by promoting vasodilation, reducing peripheral resistance, and improving overall cardiovascular function. By promoting a healthy cardiovascular system, European mistletoe capsules can help manage hypertension and support overall heart health.
remedy_properties: Vasodilation: European mistletoe contains compounds such as viscotoxins and lectins that can cause the relaxation of blood vessels, leading to decreased blood pressure. This is important for hypertension as narrowed or constricted blood vessels can increase blood pressure.

Anti-inflammatory: Mistletoe has anti-inflammatory properties due to the presence of polyphenols and flavonoids. These compounds can help reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to hypertension.

Antioxidant: Mistletoe is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect blood vessels from damage caused by free radicals. This is important for hypertension as damaged blood vessels can contribute to high blood pressure.

Cardioprotective: European mistletoe has been shown to have a positive effect on heart function and can help regulate heartbeat. This is important for hypertension as a healthy heart is crucial for maintaining normal blood pressure.

Diuretic: Mistletoe has diuretic properties, which can help increase urine production and remove excess fluid from the body. This is important for hypertension as excess fluid can increase blood volume and blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain several active compounds, including alkaloids, which can help to lower blood pressure.
Stems: Also contain the same active compounds as the leaves, and are often used in combination with the leaves for medicinal purposes.
Berries: While not typically used in capsule form, the berries of the European mistletoe plant contain lectins, which have been shown to have a hypotensive effect.
Bark: The bark of the mistletoe plant contains a number of active compounds, including viscosum alkaloids, which have been shown to have a positive effect on blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh European mistletoe (Viscum album) branches, ensuring they are harvested sustainably and legally.
Thoroughly clean the mistletoe, discarding any damaged or discolored parts, then allow it to air dry for 3-5 days.
Grind the dried mistletoe into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle, aiming for a consistency similar to commercial herbal powders.
Measure out 500mg of mistletoe powder per capsule, using a scale for accuracy, and fill size "00" vegetarian capsules.

Store the completed capsules in a cool, dry, and dark place, consuming 1-2 capsules daily as a dietary supplement for hypertension management, preferably under the guidance of a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Arjuna capsule helps with hypertension because it contains the extract of the bark of Terminalia arjuna, a tree that has been used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine for centuries. This herb has been found to have a positive impact on cardiovascular health. Arjuna is rich in coenzyme Q10, minerals, and antioxidants that help strengthen the heart muscles, improve blood circulation, and lower blood pressure. It also helps to reduce the stiffness of the blood vessels, thus promoting smooth blood flow and reducing the risk of hypertension. Furthermore, Arjuna has been shown to have a calming effect on the mind, which can help reduce stress and anxiety, both of which are contributing factors to hypertension.
remedy_properties: Cardioprotective property: Arjuna capsules contain active constituents like tannins, saponins, and flavonoids that help protect the heart from damage, improving its functioning and thereby reducing the risk of hypertension.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in Arjuna capsules, such as oligomeric proanthocyanidins, help in scavenging free radicals, reducing oxidative stress, and improving endothelial function, thereby lowering blood pressure.

Anti-inflammatory property: Arjuna capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds, such as saponins and flavonoids, that help reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to the development of hypertension.

Vasodilatory property: Arjuna capsules contain active constituents like saponins, particularly arjunolic acid, that help in dilating blood vessels, thereby reducing peripheral resistance and lowering blood pressure.
Diuretic property: Arjuna capsules have a diuretic effect, which helps in reducing fluid volume in the body, thereby lowering blood pressure and reducing the workload on the heart.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain compounds that help relax blood vessels, making it easier for the heart to pump blood and reducing hypertension.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and possess diuretic properties, helping lower blood pressure by reducing fluid volume in the body.
Seeds: Contain essential oils and fatty acids that have a positive impact on the cardiovascular system, reducing hypertension and promoting heart health.
Bark: Has anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties, helping widen blood vessels and reduce blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Grind 500g of dried Arjuna bark into a fine powder, typically taking 30 minutes.
Mix the Arjuna powder with 100g of powdered licorice root and 50g of powdered hawthorn berries.
Add just enough purified water to create a thick, uniform paste, blending for approximately 15 minutes.
Form the paste into small, equal-sized capsules, filling about 100-120 capsules, which may take around 45 minutes.
Dry the filled capsules in a cool, dark place for 24 hours before storing in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: European olive capsule helps with hypertension because it contains hydroxytyrosol, a polyphenol found in olive leaves that has been shown to have blood pressure-lowering effects. This compound works by reducing inflammation, improving endothelial function, and increasing nitric oxide production, all of which contribute to healthy blood pressure levels. Additionally, European olive capsules may help to improve lipid profiles, reduce oxidative stress, and decrease the risk of cardiovascular disease, making them a valuable tool in the management of hypertension and overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Antihypertensive effect: European olive capsules contain oleuropein, which has been shown to lower blood pressure in hypertensive individuals.
Anti-inflammatory effect: The presence of oleocanthal in European olive capsules helps reduce inflammation, which is associated with the development and progression of hypertension.

Antioxidant effect: The high polyphenol content in European olive capsules, such as hydroxytyrosol, provides antioxidant properties that can help protect blood vessels from oxidative stress and damage, thereby reducing the risk of hypertension.
Endothelial function improvement: European olive capsules can improve endothelial function by increasing nitric oxide availability, promoting vasodilation and lowering blood pressure.

Lipid-lowering effect: European olive capsules can help lower LDL cholesterol and triglyceride levels, reducing the risk of atherosclerosis and improving overall cardiovascular health in hypertensive individuals.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Olive leaves contain oleuropein, a compound that has been shown to help lower blood pressure.
Seeds: Olive seeds are rich in antioxidants and have been used in medicinal capsules to help reduce hypertension.
Bark: The bark of the olive tree contains valuable compounds such as elenolic acid, which has been studied for its potential to lower blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Combine 2 grams each of dried hawthorn, lemon balm, and European olive leaf in a bowl.
Add 500mg of crushed garlic cloves and mix the herbs well.
Fill size 00 gelatin capsules with 500mg of the herbal mixture, using a capsule filling machine.
Allow the capsules to dry for 24 hours in a cool, dark place.
Store the finished European olive capsules in an airtight container, in a cool, dry location, and take 1-2 capsules daily as needed for hypertension management.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cinnamon capsule helps with hypertension because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce blood pressure. The compound called cinnamaldehyde found in cinnamon may help dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing strain on the heart. Additionally, cinnamon has been shown to reduce levels of total cholesterol, LDL (bad) cholesterol, and triglycerides, all of which can contribute to hypertension. By incorporating cinnamon capsules into a healthy lifestyle, individuals with hypertension may be able to better manage their blood pressure and improve their overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Anti-hypertensive: Cinnamon contains antioxidants, such as polyphenols, that have been shown to help reduce high blood pressure, making it effective in managing hypertension.

Vasodilator: The compound cinnamaldehyde in cinnamon helps relax blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing the strain on the cardiovascular system, which is crucial for controlling hypertension.

Anti-inflammatory: Cinnamon has anti-inflammatory properties due to its active ingredients, including cinnamaldehyde and eugenol, which can help alleviate inflammation in blood vessels, promoting better blood pressure control.

Blood sugar regulation: Cinnamon helps lower blood sugar levels by increasing insulin sensitivity and glucose uptake in the body, which can contribute to better blood pressure management, as high blood sugar is linked to hypertension.

Antioxidant-rich: Cinnamon's high antioxidant content, such as polyphenols and flavonoids, can help protect the cardiovascular system from oxidative stress and damage, supporting overall heart health and effective hypertension management.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the cinnamon tree is commonly used in medicinal capsules for hypertension due to its richness in antioxidants and properties that help improve blood flow.
Seeds: Cinnamon seeds contain essential oils and compounds that can aid in reducing high blood pressure, making them a valuable component in hypertension treatments.
Leaves: The leaves of the cinnamon plant have anti-inflammatory properties and are used in capsules for hypertension, as they can help relax blood vessels and improve circulation.
Stems: Cinnamon stems are a source of type-A polyphenols, which have been shown to help reduce blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health, making them useful for hypertension treatment.
remedy_recipe: Grind 2 teaspoons of cinnamon bark and 1 teaspoon of hawthorn berries into a fine powder, requiring 10-15 minutes of preparation.
Mix the ground herbs with 1 teaspoon of powdered ginger and 1/2 teaspoon of powdered licorice root.
Add just enough gelatin or vegetarian capsule material to form 500mg capsules, using a capsule-making tool.
Fill a total of 20-25 capsules with the herbal mixture, allowing 20-30 minutes for completion.
Store the cinnamon capsules in a cool, dry place, and take 1-2 capsules daily as a natural supplement for hypertension management.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha capsule helps with hypertension because it contains powerful adaptogenic properties that can help reduce cortisol levels and stress, which are both linked to high blood pressure. The herb has been shown to relax blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing the force on artery walls, thereby lowering overall blood pressure. Additionally, ashwagandha has been found to have a positive impact on the endothelium, the lining of the blood vessels, promoting their health and proper function. These effects make ashwagandha a valuable supplement for individuals seeking a natural approach to managing hypertension.
remedy_properties: Anti-hypertensive: Ashwagandha has been shown to have a direct effect on reducing blood pressure, helping to manage hypertension. This property is due to the presence of alkaloids and withanolides, such as withaferin A and withanolide D.

Adaptogenic: Ashwagandha helps the body adapt to stress and maintain balance, which can help regulate high blood pressure caused by stress and anxiety. The active constituents responsible for this property are withanolides.

Anti-inflammatory: Ashwagandha has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system, contributing to improved blood pressure. The anti-inflammatory effects are attributed to withaferin A and other withanolides.

Cardioprotective: Ashwagandha has been shown to protect the heart and cardiovascular system from damage, helping to maintain overall cardiovascular health and regulate blood pressure. The cardioprotective effects are due to the presence of withanolides and steroidal lactones.

Anxiolytic: Ashwagandha can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which can help lower blood pressure in individuals with hypertension caused by stress and anxiety. The anxiolytic effects are attributed to withanolides, particularly withaferin A.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the ashwagandha plant are commonly used in making medicinal capsules for hypertension due to their adaptogenic properties that help reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to high blood pressure.
Leaves: The leaves of the ashwagandha plant contain active compounds that help relax blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing hypertension.
Seeds: The seeds of the ashwagandha plant are rich in bioactive compounds, including withanolides, that have been shown to lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease.
remedy_recipe: Gather 700mg of ashwagandha root powder and 300mg of hawthorn berry powder for each capsule, ensuring a total of 1000mg of herbal ingredients.
Combine the ashwagandha and hawthorn berry powders in a bowl, mixing thoroughly for even distribution.
Load the herbal mixture into size "00" empty gelatin capsules using a capsule filling machine or by hand, filling each half-capsule to its designated line.
Press the two halves of each capsule together, ensuring a secure seal and consistent dosage.
Store the completed ashwagandha-hawthorn capsules in a cool, dry, and airtight container to maintain potency until use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goat's head capsule helps with hypertension because it contains natural herbs that have been used for centuries to regulate blood pressure. One of the key ingredients, the root of the goat's head plant, has been shown to have vasodilatory effects, which means it helps widen blood vessels and improve blood flow. This, in turn, can help reduce the pressure on artery walls and lower high blood pressure. Additionally, the capsule may also contain other natural ingredients like hawthorn and motherwort, which have been found to have a calming effect on the cardiovascular system and help regulate heartbeat. Overall, the combination of these natural herbs in goat's head capsule can help manage hypertension and promote cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Vasodilation: Goat's head capsule contains steroidal saponins that act as vasodilators, helping to relax and widen blood vessels, reducing the pressure on the artery walls, and alleviating hypertension.

Diuretic effect: The capsule has diuretic properties due to the presence of alkaloids, which help increase urine production, aiding in the removal of excess fluids and salts from the body, and thus lowering blood pressure.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Goat's head capsule includes flavonoids and tannins that possess anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in blood vessels, improving blood flow, and contributing to the management of hypertension.

Antioxidant activity: The capsule contains phenolic compounds and vitamin C, which exhibit antioxidant properties, helping protect blood vessels from oxidative stress and damage, and supporting healthy blood pressure levels.

Cardioprotective effects: Goat's head capsule has cardioprotective properties due to its high content of essential fatty acids and phytosterols, which help maintain a healthy heart, lower bad cholesterol levels, and reduce the risk of hypertension-related cardiovascular diseases.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain compounds that help relax blood vessels, reducing hypertension.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants that aid in managing high blood pressure.
Flowers: Contain anti-inflammatory properties that help regulate blood pressure.
Seeds: Packed with essential oils and fatty acids that promote heart health and lower hypertension.
remedy_recipe: Crush 200g of dried goat's head herb and 150g of crushed bitter leaf into a powder, using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered herbs with 50g of powdered licorice root and 25g of powdered hawthorn berries.
Add just enough distilled water to create a thick paste, then stir until well combined.
Form the herbal paste into small capsules using a manual encapsulation machine.
Dry the completed capsules in a cool, dark place for 3-5 days before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsule helps with hypertension because it contains active compounds that can dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing the pressure on artery walls. The herb has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to treat various cardiovascular diseases, including hypertension. Studies suggest that the maidenhair tree extract can lower blood pressure by inhibiting the angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE), which is responsible for narrowing blood vessels. Furthermore, the antioxidant properties of the herb can help protect the cardiovascular system from oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting overall heart health.
remedy_properties: Vasodilation: Maidenhair tree capsules contain active constituents such as tannins and flavonoids that can cause the blood vessels to relax and widen, reducing blood pressure. This property is important for hypertension because narrow and constricted blood vessels can increase blood pressure, and vasodilation can help alleviate this.

Diuretic: Maidenhair tree capsules have a diuretic effect, which helps increase urine production and remove excess fluids and salts from the body. This property is important for hypertension because excess fluid and salt can contribute to high blood pressure, and removing them can help lower it.

Antioxidant: Maidenhair tree capsules contain antioxidants that can help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals. This property is important for hypertension because high blood pressure can cause oxidative stress and damage to blood vessels, and antioxidants can help mitigate this damage.

Anti-inflammatory: Maidenhair tree capsules have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the body. This property is important for hypertension because chronic inflammation can contribute to the development and progression of hypertension, and reducing inflammation can help manage it.

Nervous system relaxation: Maidenhair tree capsules can help relax the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety. This property is important for hypertension because stress and anxiety can contribute to high blood pressure, and reducing them can help lower blood pressure. The active constituents that give this property include triterpenes and sterols.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the maidenhair tree are used in medicinal capsules for hypertension due to their high content of active compounds that have a relaxing effect on blood vessels.
Stems: Maidenhair tree stems are used in medicinal capsules for hypertension because they contain compounds that help regulate blood pressure by reducing vascular resistance.
Seeds: The seeds of the maidenhair tree are used in medicinal capsules for hypertension due to their ability to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation.

Bark: The bark of the maidenhair tree is used in medicinal capsules for hypertension because it contains compounds that have a diuretic effect, helping to reduce fluid retention and lower blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 mature leaves from a maidenhair tree (Ginkgo biloba) and rinse them thoroughly to remove any dirt or impurities.
Chop the leaves finely and combine them with 2 teaspoons of powdered hawthorn berry and 1 teaspoon of powdered valerian root.
Blend the herbal mixture with a mortar and pestle until a uniform consistency is achieved, then transfer it to a clean capsule-making machine.
Fill size "00" vegetarian capsules with the herbal blend, using the capsule-making machine, and let them dry for 24 hours in a cool, dark place.
Take one capsule twice daily, 30 minutes before meals, for optimal blood pressure management.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bilberry capsule helps with hypertension because it contains anthocyanins, which are powerful antioxidants that help improve blood flow and strengthen blood vessel walls. This can lead to a reduction in blood pressure, making bilberry capsules a useful supplement for individuals with hypertension. Additionally, bilberry has been shown to help regulate cholesterol levels and reduce inflammation, both of which can contribute to the development and progression of hypertension. By providing a natural and holistic approach to managing high blood pressure, bilberry capsules offer a valuable alternative to traditional medications, which can come with unwanted side effects.
remedy_properties: Vasodilation: Bilberry contains anthocyanins that dilate blood vessels, reducing resistance in blood flow, which is important for hypertension as it helps lower elevated blood pressure.

Antioxidant properties: The flavonoids in bilberry, such as quercetin and myricetin, have antioxidant effects that protect blood vessels from damage, which is crucial for hypertension as it helps maintain vascular health.
Blood sugar regulation: Bilberry anthocyanins help regulate blood sugar levels, which is important for hypertension as uncontrolled blood sugar can exacerbate the condition.

Blood clot prevention: Bilberry inhibits platelet aggregation, reducing the risk of blood clots, which is significant for hypertension as it minimizes the risk of complications like strokes and heart attacks.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Bilberry exhibits anti-inflammatory properties due to its anthocyanin content, which is important for hypertension as chronic inflammation can contribute to the development and progression of hypertension.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of flavonoids, particularly anthocyanins, which have been shown to help lower blood pressure.
Berries: Rich in antioxidants and polyphenols, berries can help improve circulation and reduce inflammation, contributing to lower blood pressure.
Seeds: High in omega-3 fatty acids, seeds can help reduce inflammation and improve heart health, both of which can help lower blood pressure.
Buds: Contain high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them useful for improving cardiovascular health and reducing hypertension.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 cup of dried bilberry fruit and 1/2 cup of hawthorn berries, both known for their blood pressure-lowering properties.
Grind the bilberry and hawthorn berries into a fine powder using a high-speed blender or coffee grinder, which should take approximately 5-10 minutes.
Mix 1 tablespoon of the powdered herbal blend with 1 tablespoon of tapioca starch, which acts as a binding agent, in a small bowl.
Evenly distribute the mixture into empty gelatin capsules using a capsule-filling machine or by hand, ensuring each capsule holds an equal amount.
Seal the capsules and store in a cool, dry place until ready for use, making sure to consume the recommended dosage as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea capsule helps with hypertension because it contains antioxidants that can help relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, which can help lower high blood pressure. Additionally, white tea has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can further support cardiovascular health. White tea is also lower in caffeine compared to other teas, making it a more gentle option for those looking to manage their blood pressure. The combination of these benefits makes white tea capsules a valuable tool in maintaining healthy blood pressure levels.
remedy_properties: Vasodilation: White tea helps widen blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing the pressure on artery walls, thanks to the active compounds like catechins and flavonoids.

Antioxidant properties: White tea contains high levels of antioxidants that help protect blood vessels from damage, which is crucial for maintaining healthy blood pressure, due to the presence of polyphenols and flavonoids.

Anti-inflammatory effects: White tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in blood vessels and tissues, contributing to better blood pressure management, by the actions of tannins and caffeine.

Inhibition of angiotensin-converting enzyme: White tea can inhibit ACE, which plays a role in narrowing blood vessels and increasing blood pressure; this is attributed to the presence of epigallocatechin-3-gallate (EGCG).

Improving endothelial function: White tea supports endothelial function by promoting the release of nitric oxide, which helps relax and dilate blood vessels, due to the presence of catechins and other polyphenols.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants that help relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, reducing hypertension.
Buds: Rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health.
Stems: Contain catechins, which have been found to have a positive effect on blood pressure and overall heart health.
Bark: Has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system, improving hypertension.
Seeds: Rich in essential oils and fatty acids, which can help lower blood pressure and improve heart health.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried organic white tea leaves and 1 teaspoon of dried hawthorn berries, both known for reducing hypertension.
Grind the measured herbs in a coffee grinder for 2-3 minutes until they form a fine powder.
Mix 0.5 teaspoon of the ground herbal powder with 100mg of microcrystalline cellulose as a binding agent in a bowl.
Fill size 00 empty gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture using a capsule filling machine, and let them dry for 1 hour.
Store the finished white tea and hawthorn berry capsules in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat hypertension with herbal capsules is by using a combination of proven herbs such as hawthorn, garlic, and olive leaf extract. These herbs have been clinically shown to lower blood pressure by relaxing and dilating blood vessels, reducing inflammation, and improving overall cardiovascular health. It's essential to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new supplement regimen, as individual responses to herbs can vary. Proper dosage and consistent use are crucial for effective results. Additionally, maintaining a healthy lifestyle, including a balanced diet and regular exercise, can further support healthy blood pressure levels.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hypertension that are treated with herbal capsules include mild to moderate hypotension, arrhythmia, and atherosclerosis. Herbal supplements such as Hawthorn, valerian root, and garlic have been used to manage these conditions. Hawthorn can help regulate blood pressure, valerian root may promote a normal heart rhythm, and garlic has been shown to support healthy blood vessel function. However, it's important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new supplement regimen, especially for those with pre-existing medical conditions or those taking prescription medications.
